tutorial.markdown.formatting:
# Start Here: Markdown 80/20 Playground

Welcome to Notty. This note teaches the small set of Markdown features that gives most of the value for non-technical users.

## Markdown 80/20 (Most Useful)
- Headings: `#`, `##`, `###`
- Emphasis: `**bold**` and `*italic*`
- Lists: `- item`
- Checklists: `- [ ] task` and `- [x] done`
- Links: `[label](https://example.com)`
- Wiki links: `[[Note Title]]`
- Inline code: `` `short code` ``
- Quotes: `> important idea`

## Playground (Try It Live)
Copy this block into a new note and edit it freely:

```markdown
# My first note in Notty

## What I am working on
- Task 1
- Task 2

## Checklist
- [ ] Draft
- [ ] Review
- [ ] Finish

Useful reference: [Markdown Guide](https://www.markdownguide.org)
Related note: [[Connect Ideas with Wiki Links]]

> Tip: keep one idea per note.
```

## Next Step
Continue with [[Connect Ideas with Wiki Links]] and then [[Find Anything with Search and Tags]].

#index #tutorial #markdown #playground
tutorial.markdown.advanced:
# Connect Ideas with Wiki Links

Wiki links transform isolated notes into a connected knowledge graph.

## How it Works
Write a note title inside double brackets:

```
[[Start Here: Markdown 80/20 Playground]]
```

If the target note exists, Notty links to it automatically.
If it does not exist, it becomes a ghost link you can create later.

## Practical Pattern
- Project note links to meeting notes
- Meeting notes link to decisions
- Decisions link to action plans

Try this now:
- Link this note to [[Find Anything with Search and Tags]].
- Add one ghost link like [[Customer interview synthesis]].

#tutorial #wikilinks #knowledge_graph
tutorial.markdown.wikilinks:
# Find Anything with Search and Tags

Search is best when you remember words.
Tags are best when you remember context.

## Tag Strategy
Use a small, stable set of tags:
- #project
- #meeting
- #decision
- #reference
- #next_action

## Weekly Review (5 minutes)
- [ ] Open Inbox and assign notebooks where needed
- [ ] Merge duplicate tags
- [ ] Update your index note
- [ ] Check backlinks for orphan notes

For structure, return to [[Start Here: Markdown 80/20 Playground]].
For linking, revisit [[Connect Ideas with Wiki Links]].

#tutorial #search #tags #review
